content.text:
Now I have a very clear view of atom 24 (C) and all its covalent bonds. From this angle, I can clearly see the bonds connecting C:24 to its neighbours.

Based on my visual inspection from multiple angles, here are the **neighbour atoms of atom 24 (C)**:

| Atomic Index | Element |
|:---:|:---:|
| 21 | C |
| 25 | C |
| 29 | C |
| 34 | C |

**Explanation:** Atom C:24 is at a junction point in the molecule. It is covalently bonded to four carbon atoms:
- **C:21** — connecting toward the pyrazole/imidazole ring (upper-left)
- **C:25** — connecting to the upper ring system (upper-right)
- **C:29** — connecting to the lower cyclopentane-like ring (lower-left)
- **C:34** — connecting to the fused aromatic ring (lower-right)

**geomconf_index: 4846** (derived from the filename `geomconf_4846.xyz`)

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 24, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
